introduction.text: Time to relax a bit. We are talking about 'Soft Hands' – hands with an Ace counted as 11. Why are they cool? Because you CANNOT Bust with one card! If you go over 21, the Ace simply changes to 1. It's like having an extra life. Let's learn how to use it.

scenarios.title: The Soft 17 Myth (A,6 vs 7)
scenarios.goal: Teach that Soft 17 is a weak hand that must be hit.

sequence.text: You have Soft 17 (Ace+6). Many players Stand here. Wrong! 17 is not enough to beat the Dealer comfortably. Since you cannot bust, you MUST try to improve. Hit!
Now you have 20. Much better than 17. Stand.

finish.text: Look! You drew a 3 and now have 20. You beat my 17. If you had stood on Soft 17, it would be a tie (Push). Always Hit Soft 17.

scenarios.title: Soft 13 Safety (A,2 vs 8)
scenarios.goal: Demonstrate the safety of hitting low soft hands.

sequence.text: You have Soft 13 (Ace+2). It's either 3 or 13. You have absolutely nothing to lose. Hit to improve.
You drew a Queen (10). 13 + 10 = 23? No! The Ace becomes 1. So 1 + 2 + 10 = 13. You are back to where you started (Hard 13). Now play it like a Hard Hand vs 8. Hit again!
You got a 6. Total 19. Now you are safe. Stand.

finish.text: Great recovery. You ended up with 19.

scenarios.title: The Tricky Soft 18 (A,7 vs 9)
scenarios.goal: Teach hitting Soft 18 against strong dealer cards.

sequence.text: You have Soft 18. 18 feels good, but I have a 9 (likely 19). You are behind. Since you cannot bust, you must Hit Soft 18 against 9, 10, or Ace.

finish.text: Perfect. You improved to 20 and won. Standing on 18 would have lost.

sequence.text: Soft 19 (Ace+8). This is a monster hand. Never Hit this. Just take the probable win or tie.

finish.text: It's a Push (Tie). 19 vs 19. Good game.

sequence.text: Soft 20. Do not overthink. Do not Double. Just Stand and smile.

conclusion.text: Now you understand the safety of Soft Hands. But remember, sometimes 'safety' is boring. In the next lesson, we will learn when to ATTACK with Soft Hands using Double Down!
